functions.label: extractLiquidErrorPosition
description:
Extracts position information from liquidjs error messages
By default, liquidjs returns the start of the line of the error message
This function tries to pinpoint the specific problematic token rather than just the start of the expression
signature: (text: string, errorMessage: string) => { start: number; end: number; }

functions.label: formatZodError
description:
Formats Zod validation errors into user-friendly messages.
Uses schema-aware enrichment to provide helpful hints about expected values.

functions.label: getCompactTypeDescription
description: Get a compact type description suitable for tooltips

functions.label: getDetailedTypeDescription
description: Generate a detailed human-readable type description from a Zod schema

functions.label: getLiquidInstance
description:
Returns the shared workflow Liquid engine instance (lazy-initialized).
Registers stub filters so that parse() does not throw on known filter names.

functions.label: inferZodType
description: Infer a zod schema from an object.
signature: (obj: unknown, { isConst = false }: { isConst?: boolean | undefined; }) =>

children.label: obj
description: - The object to infer the schema from.

functions.label: isDynamicValue
isLiquidTagValue
description:
Checks if a value contains Liquid tag patterns ({% ... %} or {%- ... -%})
Examples: {% if condition %}, {%- if condition -%}, multi-line blocks with Liquid tags
Pattern: matches {% or {%- followed by content and %} or -%}
The 's' flag allows . to match newlines for multi-line support

functions.label: isVariableValue
parseTemplateString
description:
Parses a Liquid template string and caches the result.
Uses a bounded LRU cache: when the cache exceeds MAX_PARSE_CACHE_SIZE,
the least-recently-used entry is evicted.

functions.label: parseYamlToJSONWithoutValidation
description: Parse a YAML string to a JSON object. This function is dangerous because it does not use schema to validate the resulting JSON object.

children.label: yamlString
description: - The YAML string to parse.

returnComment:
The JSON object and the YAML document. The document is always
available regardless of success/failure because `parseDocument` is
error-tolerant.

functions.label: stringifyWorkflowDefinition
description: Stringify the workflow definition to a YAML string.

children.label: workflowDefinition
description: - The workflow definition as a JSON object.

children.label: sortKeys
description: - Whether to sort the keys of the workflow definition.

returnComment: The YAML string of the workflow definition.

functions.label: updateYamlField
description:
Updates a field in YAML while preserving comments, formatting, and blank lines.
This is useful when updating workflow metadata (like enabled, name, description, tags)
without losing user's formatting and comments.
signature: (yamlString: string, fieldPath: string, value: unknown) => string

children.label: yamlString
description: - The original YAML string

children.label: fieldPath
description: - The dot-notated path to the field to update (e.g., 'enabled', 'name', 'parent.child')

children.label: value
description: - The new value for the field

returnComment: The updated YAML string with formatting preserved

children.label: connectorParamsSchemaResolver
description:
Optional resolver that returns the params schema for a given connector/step
type. When omitted, connector-specific error enrichment is skipped and
enrichment falls back to the workflow schema.

children.label: schema
description: Optional workflow schema for enhanced error messages

children.label: yamlDocument

children.label: connectorParamsSchemaResolver

objects.label: LIQUID_BLOCK_FILTER_REGEX
description:
LIQUID_BLOCK_FILTER_REGEX matches a variable followed by a filter within a Liquid block, but outside mustache syntax.

Regex breakdown:
(?:^|[^{])      - Non-capturing group: start of line or any character except '{' (to avoid mustache blocks)
\s*             - Optional whitespace
[^{}\s]+        - One or more characters that are not '{', '}', or whitespace (the variable name)
\s*             - Optional whitespace
\|              - Pipe character separating variable and filter
\s*             - Optional whitespace
(\w*)           - Capturing group: the filter name (alphanumeric/underscore)
\s*$            - Optional whitespace to end of line

Example match: "foo | filter"
